summary: Build (tsc --noEmit) and tests (115/115) pass. However, there are 5 critical runtime bugs in goal-repo.ts and types/db.ts: (1) goal-repo.ts still references 'goal_tasks' and 'goal_task_deps' tables which were renamed to '_deprecated_*' by migration 010 → runtime 'no such table' errors; (2) SQL uses @drive_thread_id but JS provides drive_channel_id → goalChannelId silently saved as NULL; (3) SQL INSERT uses @thread_id but JS provides channel_id → task channelId saved as NULL; (4) GoalRow.drive_channel_id TypeScript type doesn't match DB column drive_thread_id → reads return undefined; (5) GoalTaskRow.channel_id doesn't match old table's thread_id column. These bugs would break all Goal orchestrator save/load operations at runtime.

issues.description: Table name mismatch: SQL references 'goal_tasks' (line 68) and 'goal_task_deps' (line 71), but migration 010 renamed these to '_deprecated_goal_tasks' and '_deprecated_goal_task_deps'. After migration runs, GoalRepo.get() and GoalRepo.save() will fail with 'no such table: goal_tasks'. The correct table names are 'tasks' and 'task_deps'. This also affects lines 100, 104, 120, and 162.

SQL binding mismatch: upsertGoal SQL uses @drive_thread_id (lines 42, 47, 55) but goalDriveStateToGoalRow() returns { drive_channel_id: state.goalChannelId } (line 191). better-sqlite3 binds by key name, so @drive_thread_id resolves to undefined → NULL. Goal's goalChannelId is silently lost on every save. Fix: change JS key back to 'drive_thread_id' since the DB column was never renamed by migration 010.

SQL column/binding mismatch: INSERT INTO goal_tasks uses column 'thread_id' and binding @thread_id (line 112), but the run() object uses key 'channel_id' (line 133). The new 'tasks' table has column 'channel_id', not 'thread_id'. When switching to 'tasks' table, use @channel_id binding. If keeping old table, use @thread_id key in JS object.

GoalRow.drive_channel_id doesn't match actual DB column name 'drive_thread_id' (from migration 001, never altered). SELECT * returns { drive_thread_id: ... }, so GoalRow.drive_channel_id will be undefined. rowsToGoalDriveState() reads goal.drive_channel_id (goal-repo.ts:229) → always empty string. Fix: keep TS property as 'drive_thread_id' (matching DB), or add ALTER TABLE in migration.

GoalTaskRow.channel_id (line 177) doesn't match old 'goal_tasks' table column 'thread_id'. If goal-repo.ts reads from old table, rows will have thread_id not channel_id. If it reads from new 'tasks' table, channel_id is correct. This must be consistent with which table goal-repo.ts queries.
notify() method parameter still named 'threadId'. Internal refs at lines 2825, 2832, 2837 also use 'threadId'. Should be 'channelId'.
HandleReplanDeps.notify callback parameter still named 'threadId'. Should be 'channelId'.
Fire-and-forget async without .catch(): claudeSessionRepo.getActiveByChannel().then() has no error handler (lines 337, 482). Unhandled promise rejections.
